channel.title: Having all css in js in separate files referenced on page
channel.description: I really dislike have css code on the js pages themselves. Is it bad practice to write the css in js components in a style folder and then reference that on the page?

item.title: Having all css in js in separate files referenced on page
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Hi @ekline ,

Welcome to the Community!

ekline:

Is it bad practice to write the css in js components in a style folder and then reference that on the page?

Frontity projects are like any other Isomorphic React Apps so you can organize your code in the way that makes more sense for you.

As a reference, here you have a project that uses separated files for the styles of each component

github.com

frontity-demos/bootstrap-theme-demo

Contribute to frontity-demos/bootstrap-theme-demo development by creating an account on GitHub.

This is the file’s structure of the project where you can see the combination of index.js + styles.js for each component

packages
└── bootstrap-theme
    ├── CHANGELOG.md
    ├── package.json
    └── src
        ├── components
        │   ├── FeaturedMedia
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── Header
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── Link
        │   │   └── index.js
        │   ├── List
        │   │   └── index.js
        │   ├── ListBlock
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── ListItem
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── ListPagination
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── Loading
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── MenuIcons
        │   │   └── index.js
        │   ├── MenuModal
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── MobileMenu
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── Nav
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── PageError
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── Post
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   ├── Theme
        │   │   ├── index.js
        │   │   └── styles.js
        │   └── Title
        │       └── index.js
        ├── helpers
        │   ├── css.js
        │   └── index.js
        └── index.js

As @harpreet.freelance suggests

harpreet.freelance:

you can make 1 css file which is .js file and import it

the styles are imported (as React components generated with Styled Components) in the index.js of each component

import {MenuOverlay, MenuContent, MenuLink} from './styles'

Hope this helps

Read full topic
item.source: Having all css in js in separate files referenced on page

item.title: Having all css in js in separate files referenced on page
item.dc:creator: harpreet.freelance
item.description:
For reference you can check in frontity twenty twenty theme styles folder

item.title: Having all css in js in separate files referenced on page
item.dc:creator: harpreet.freelance
item.description:
yes you can make 1 css file which is .js file and import it where you want.

Read full topic
